title: Pseudoclausena chrysogyne
taxonomy: Pseudoclausena chrysogyne belongs to the family Rutaceae and the genus Pseudoclausena. The species is a flowering plant native to Madagascar. It is classified as a small tree or shrub. Pseudoclausena chrysogyne is part of the order Sapindales and the class Magnoliopsida. The plant's classification reflects its botanical relationships and characteristics.

intro: Pseudoclausena chrysogyne, also known as the golden-flowered Pseudoclausena, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, commonly found in tropical regions. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Pseudoclausena chrysogyne include limonoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Pseudoclausena chrysogyne is often used to prepare infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are used to treat conditions such as fever, cough, and digestive disorders.
